topic: Two roommates disagree about getting a pet
raw_output:
[story 1]

The sheer thought of a creature sharing my cramped living space made my stomach clench. I stared at Leo, who was practically vibrating with excitement over the idea of adopting a parrot. "Think of the conversation starter, Maya! A feathered companion!" he chirped, gesturing wildly with his hands. I just leaned back against the worn sofa, crossing my arms tightly over my chest. A faint tremor ran through my hands, and I couldn't meet his eyes. "Leo, I just... I don't see how that fits into this apartment," I managed, my voice coming out tight and brittle. The air in the room suddenly felt thick, suffocating, and I instinctively took a sharp, shallow breath.

[story 2]

I watched Clara carefully as she paced the kitchen floor, her energy radiating an almost manic enthusiasm for a hamster. Every time she spoke about the tiny cage and the constant squeaking, a cold knot tightened in my throat. I kept wiping down the already spotless counter, my movements becoming overly precise, almost obsessive. "It'll be so cute, Maya! A little furry friend to keep you company," Clara insisted, her voice pitched high with eagerness. I stopped scrubbing, letting my cloth drop onto the counter with a soft, heavy thud. I felt a distinct urge to physically recoil, a tightening sensation in my jaw that made my teeth ache. "Clara, I'm just not comfortable with that kind of mess," I said, my tone flat, devoid of any warmth.

[story 3]

The proposal hung in the air like a heavy, unpleasant odor. Liam was practically beaming, describing the gentle nature of a snake he’d seen on a documentary. I felt a wave of nausea wash over me, making my palms instantly slick. I took a deliberate step backward, bumping lightly into the doorframe. "A reptile? Liam, seriously?" My voice was surprisingly low, a strained monotone. I looked down at my shoes, focusing intently on the scuffs on the floorboards. The excitement in his eyes seemed to grate on my nerves, making my skin crawl. "I just... I can't handle that kind of responsibility," I mumbled, avoiding any direct engagement with his hopeful expression.
